title: starting a project, being creative, solving a problem

cards.title: Crystal Ball Technique
cards.content:
*imagine that something has gone wrong, analyze why*

> Next, try this exercise another way. Take out another piece of paper and sit back in your chair. Think about the plan for gathering the team members together. How nice that the schedules all fit together. But now we are going to look into a crystal ball to see the future and . . . we see that the plan was a fiasco. The meeting never happened. The project lost direction and acquired a black eye. Unfortunately, the crystal ball doesn’t show why the meeting got derailed.

— 📖 Power of Full Engagement

cards.title: ~ start painful projects with half an hour of brainstorming
cards.content:
> brainstorming under a tree is romantic, and therefore much easier to actually do.

— [The Ice Bath Method﹕ Easing Into Painful Projects](https://www.calnewport.com/blog/2009/09/22/the-ice-bath-method-easing-into-painful-projects/)

cards.title: ~ analysis paralysis is dangerous in strategy searching
cards.content:
> One reason that we avoid choosing a strategy is that we’re not comfortable walking away from all the other possible strategies. Rather than celebrate the paths not taken, we take no path at all. 

— 📖 This Is Strategy

cards.title: ~ ask﹕ who are you seeking to change﹖
~ first, write down a clear problem definition
